heading: Reports by and examinations of nonbank financial companies by the Board of Governors
label: 5361 Reports by and examinations of nonbank financial companies by the Board of Governors

Rule of Construction Regarding No New Disclosure RequirementsAmendment by not to be construed to require certain additional information to be collected or disclosed, see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 117–263section 5864 of Pub. L. 117–263section 253 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the financial condition of the company or subsidiary, systems of the company or subsidiary for monitoring and controlling financial, operating, and other risks, and the extent to which the activities and operations of the company or subsidiary pose a threat to the financial stability of the United States; and

children.label: B
children.text: compliance by the company or subsidiary with the requirements of this subchapter.

children.heading: Use of existing reports and information
children.label: 2 Use of existing reports and information
A
children.text: reports and supervisory information that a nonbank financial company or subsidiary thereof has been required to provide to other Federal or State regulatory agencies;

children.label: B
children.text: information otherwise obtainable from Federal or State regulatory agencies;

children.label: C
children.text: information that is otherwise required to be reported publicly; and

children.label: D
children.text: externally audited financial statements of such company or subsidiary.

children.heading: Availability
children.label: 3 Availability
children.text: Upon the request of the Board of Governors, a nonbank financial company supervised by the Board of Governors, or a subsidiary thereof, shall promptly provide to the Board of Governors any information described in paragraph (2).

children.heading: Data standards for reports under this subsection
children.label: 4 Data standards for reports under this subsection

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Board of Governors shall adopt data standards for all information that, through a collection of information, is regularly filed with or submitted to the Board of Governors under this subsection by any nonbank financial company supervised by the Board of Governors or any subsidiary thereof.

children.heading: Consistency
children.label: B Consistency
children.text: The data standards required under subparagraph (A) shall incorporate, and ensure compatibility with (to the extent feasible), all applicable data standards established in the rules promulgated under , including, to the extent practicable, by having the characteristics described in clauses (i) through (vi) of subsection (c)(1)(B) of .section 5334 of this titlesection 5334 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the nature of the operations and financial condition of the company and such subsidiary;

children.label: B
children.text: the financial, operational, and other risks of the company or such subsidiary that may pose a threat to the safety and soundness of such company or subsidiary or to the financial stability of the United States;

children.label: C
children.text: the systems for monitoring and controlling such risks; and

children.label: D
children.text: compliance by the company or such subsidiary with the requirements of this subchapter.

children.heading: Use of examination reports and information
children.label: 2 Use of examination reports and information
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the Board of Governors shall, to the fullest extent possible, rely on reports of examination of any subsidiary depository institution or functionally regulated subsidiary made by the primary financial regulatory agency for that subsidiary, and on information described in subsection (a)(2).

children.heading: Coordination with primary financial regulatory agency
children.label: c Coordination with primary financial regulatory agency

children.text: provide reasonable notice to, and consult with, the primary financial regulatory agency for any subsidiary before requiring a report or commencing an examination of such subsidiary under this section; and
avoid duplication of examination activities, reporting requirements, and requests for information, to the fullest extent possible.
